# UC-22.2.31 · NIS2 Entity Classification Validation (Essential vs Important)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Beginner &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We double-check that the big organisation chart labels—who counts as "extra supervised" versus "regular supervised"—match what the inventory system thinks, so nobody follows the wrong rulebook by accident.*

---

## Description

Compares the legal NIS2 tier recorded in the classification lookup against CMDB tags synced from ServiceNow so mislabelled legal entities and the servers attached to them surface before reporting deadlines slip.

## Value

Prevents the wrong regulatory playbook—essential versus important—from silently propagating through automation by catching CMDB drift the week it happens, not when a supervisor asks for evidence months later.

## Implementation

(1) Legal publishes authoritative tiers into `nis2_entity_classification.csv`; block edits without CAB ticket. (2) ServiceNow CMDB business rules write `nis2_entity_tag` nightly. (3) Save `UC-22.2.31` daily. (4) Auto-create ServiceNow tasks for mismatches. (5) Map `nis2_entity_scope.csv` during cross-border reviews.

## Evidence

`UC-22.2.31` mismatch report with `legal_entity_id`, snapshot of CMDB tags, ServiceNow task numbers, and signed PDF in `audit_evidence` referencing Art.2(1) classification checks.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Set `legal_entity_id=LE-TEST` to `entity_tier=essential` in the lookup while CMDB events carry `nis2_entity_tag=important` for the same ID; run the search and confirm a row with `mismatch=1`.

### Negative scenario

Align CMDB `nis2_entity_tag` text to include essential per the lookup (or remove the CI test data) and verify the next run produces no row for that entity.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

- **Splunk**: Enterprise ≥9.2 or Splunk Cloud (current). Scheduling requires a search head not under memory pressure (check `index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=ProcessRunner` for OOM warnings before deploying a new scheduled search).
- **Technology Add-on**: Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928) must be installed on search heads and indexers. Verify with `| rest /services/apps/local | search label="Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow" | table label version`.
- **Indexes**: `cmdb` must exist on all indexers with retention matching the regulation's minimum (PCI: 1y, HIPAA: 6y, NIST 800-53: 1-3y per FIPS 199, SOX: 7y). Set `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` accordingly in `indexes.conf`.
- **Lookups**: `nis2_entity_classification.csv` must be deployed to the search-tier app and refreshed on a documented schedule. For KV Store lookups, verify with `| rest /servicesNS/-/-/data/transforms/lookups | search name IN ('nis2_entity_classification.csv')`.
- **Regulatory mapping**: This detection evidences clause `Art.2(1)`. The control objective is: "Evidence that NIS2 Art.2(1) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.2.31: NIS2 Entity Classification Validation (Essential vs Important)." — every alert payload should include this text so downstream consumers know the compliance purpose.

## Step 1 — Configure data collection

Data collection is performed by the Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase app ID 1928), landing events in `cmdb` under `snow:cmdb_ci_server`. REST-polled ticket tables produce 500-5000 records per sync per table; at 300s interval expect ~3-8 GB/month for a mid-size service desk.

Configure the input through Settings > Data Inputs > ServiceNow Service Account. A reference `inputs.conf` stanza looks like:

```ini
[snow://{instance_slug}]
url = https://<instance>.service-now.com
table = {table_name}
interval = 300
index = itsm
sourcetype = {sourcetype}
```

The following fields are authoritative for this detection once the TA parses them: `number, state, priority, assignment_group, opened_at, closed_at, sys_updated_on, caller_id, category, subcategory`. Verify they are extracted at search time with `index=cmdb earliest=-15m | fieldsummary` before scheduling the detection.


5 operational considerations apply to the rollout of this detection:


- **Hint 1:** Legal publishes authoritative tiers into `nis2_entity_classification.csv`; block edits without CAB ticket. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

- **Hint 2:** ServiceNow CMDB business rules write `nis2_entity_tag` nightly. CIM-compliance tagging must be validated end-to-end: a downstream datamodel that does not accelerate because of a missing tag is a silent failure.

- **Hint 3:** Save `UC-22.2.31` daily. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

- **Hint 4:** Auto-create ServiceNow tasks for mismatches. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

- **Hint 5:** Map `nis2_entity_scope.csv` during cross-border reviews. This is an operational consideration that influences either the detection's accuracy or its maintainability — document the approach chosen in the UC runbook.

## Step 2 — Implement the search

For "NIS2 Entity Classification Validation (Essential vs Important)," the aggregation produces the result shape required by the NIS2 control — each row is a summary record the regulation expects to be presentable at audit time.

This search begins with `| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv` — the base dataset is a CSV/KV Store lookup, not live event data. This inverts the usual Splunk assumption: the detection does not stream from an index, it iterates a register. The register itself is the authoritative source of truth (contracts, policies, sign-offs, attestations) and the search joins it against indexed events to find gaps.

Lookup authority: treat `nis2_entity_classification.csv` as a primary compliance artifact. Its contents ARE the answer the regulator will ask for ('show me your vendor list,' 'show me your CDE inventory,' 'show me your policy exceptions'). Implement the following controls on the lookup itself, not just the search:

- Version the CSV in a git repository with a mandatory pull-request review for every change.
- Maintain a signed-record trail: who changed what, when, and with what approval. A lookup with a silent edit history is an audit liability.
- Automate a nightly diff: `| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv | eval hash=md5(...) | outputlookup append=true nis2_entity_classification_audit.csv` — this produces an immutable record of the register's state over time.
- Lock the lookup with role-based access: only the content owner has `list_inputs` + `write_file` on the path; everyone else is read-only.

The live-data side of the join (across the last 1 day) is the detection trigger — when the register and the telemetry disagree, that's a finding.

**Schedule configuration** (`savedsearches.conf`):
```ini
[UC-22.2.31: NIS2 Entity Classification Validation (Essential vs Importan]
cron_schedule = 0 1 * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -1d
dispatch.latest_time = now
actions = summary_index
action.summary_index._name = summary_compliance
action.summary_index.marker = uc=22.2.31,reg=NIS2
```

## Step 3 — Validate deployment

**(a) Field extraction check:** Run the following to confirm the TA is extracting every field the detection relies on:

```spl
index=cmdb sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci_server earliest=-1h
| fieldsummary
| where count=0
| table field
```

Any row returned is a field the TA claims to extract but isn't appearing in recent data — it's either a rename upstream, a TA version mismatch, or a data-format change.

**(b) Required-field presence:** This detection specifically needs: `cmdb_tags`, `entity_tier`, `legal_entity_id`, `mismatch`. Run:

```spl
index=cmdb sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci_server earliest=-1h
| head 100
| table cmdb_tags, entity_tier, legal_entity_id, mismatch
```

Every row should have non-null values for the fields the detection's `where` clause filters on. Nulls cause silent drops in the result set.

**(e) Negative test:** Generate a synthetic 'should not alert' event using the Splunk Sample Generator or a crafted `| makeresults` event and confirm the detection does NOT fire on it. A detection that fires on everything is equivalent to a detection that fires on nothing.

## Step 4 — Operationalize (dashboard and reporting)

Create a companion dashboard titled `UC-22.2.31: NIS2 evidence` with the following panel layout:

**Panel 1 — Table Of Mismatched Entities (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 2 — Single Value Count Needing Review (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.

**Panel 3 — Optional Sankey From Entity To Ci Counts (the key fields):** Configure according to standard Splunk visualization patterns for this panel type.


Time picker defaults: set to last 30 days with named presets for 24h, 7d, 30d, 90d, and 1y — the auditor needs all five windows without retyping. Enable scheduled PDF export for the full dashboard on the regulation's reporting cadence.

## Step 5 — Troubleshooting and regulation-specific caveats

**Search returns no results when violations are expected:** Verify time-window alignment, index existence, and field extractions in that order. Most 'missing results' are simpler configuration issues before they're detection-logic issues.

**Search times out:** Check the search job inspector (`Activity > Jobs > <this UC's last run> > Inspect`). The phase consuming the time (fetch, command, summary) indicates whether the issue is data-layer (too wide a window, unaccelerated datamodel), command-layer (expensive `transaction` or `eventstats`), or post-aggregation.


**NIS2-specific failure modes:**

- 24/7 reporting obligation (Art. 23): NIS2 requires early warning within 24 hours of awareness of a significant incident. Detections that back this SLA must compute elapsed time from the SOC's first-acknowledged timestamp (not the resolve-time) and must trigger independently of business hours — the regulatory clock does not pause overnight.

- Member-state variation: NIS2 is transposed individually by each EU Member State with some latitude on thresholds and sector scoping. A detection correct for German IT-SiG 3 (BSI) may be misaligned with Dutch CWV or French LPM. Use `nis2_member_state_config.csv` to parametrize thresholds per jurisdiction; never hardcode.

- Essential vs Important entity criteria: the applicable obligations depend on sector + size. Before alerting, verify the monitored entity's designation; a detection firing on an out-of-scope subsidiary causes unnecessary regulatory notifications that erode trust with the national CSIRT.

- Supply-chain (Art. 21(2)(d)) extent: NIS2 explicitly includes direct suppliers' cybersecurity posture. Detections must cover monitored-by-you-not-owned-by-you infrastructure (outsourced SaaS, managed SIEM tenants) — these are in scope even if operationally run by a third party. BA/BAA-style governance applies.

## SPL

```spl
| inputlookup nis2_entity_classification.csv
| join legal_entity_id type=left [
    search index=cmdb sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci_server earliest=-1d
    | stats values(nis2_entity_tag) as cmdb_tags by legal_entity_id
]
| eval mismatch=if(isnull(cmdb_tags),1,if(NOT match(mvjoin(cmdb_tags,"|"), entity_tier),1,0))
| where mismatch=1
| table legal_entity_id, entity_tier, cmdb_tags, mismatch
```

## Visualization

Table of mismatched entities, single value count needing review, optional Sankey from entity to CI counts.

## Known False Positives

• **CMDB lag after legal reclassification** — Distinguish: court filing updated but SNOW import still queued **24 hours**. Suppress: time-bound `nis2_classification_transition_register.csv` with `legal_effective_date`.
• **Multi-tier holdings in one legal entity** — Distinguish: `entity_tier` aggregate while CMDB tags list subsidiaries. Suppress: enrich with `mvappend` from lookup `nis2_subsidiary_map.csv` before `match()`.
• **Case-sensitive tags** — Distinguish: `Essential` vs `essential`. Suppress: `| eval entity_tier=lower(entity_tier)` pipeline macro.
• **Test CIs in production CI classes** — Distinguish: `host.class=test` still inherits stale tag. Suppress: `where NOT match(name,"^tst")` filter on CMDB subsearch.
• **Assetless legal shell** — Distinguish: no cmdb rows yet for brand-new SPV. Suppress: allow `mismatch=1` but route to legal queue not ops via lookup `nis2_zero_asset_entities.csv`.
